title: A dataset recorded during development of an affective brain-computer music interface: training sessions
description: This dataset contains electroencephalogram (EEG), galvanic skin response (GSR), and electrocardiogram (ECG) recordings from 17 healthy participants during an affective music brain-computer interface training study. Participants listened to 40-second music clips (20 seconds per emotional state) designed to induce specific emotional states across three sessions, with self-reported valence and arousal ratings. The data supports the development and validation of music-based brain-computer interfaces for monitoring and inducing affective states. This dataset is part of a three-dataset series that also includes system calibration and online real-time control sessions.
methods_description: EEG, GSR, and ECG data were recorded at 1 kHz sampling rate from 17 participants listening to synthetic music clips (40 s duration) generated using a synthetic music generator. Each clip targeted two affective states sequentially: 20 seconds for emotional state A followed by 20 seconds for emotional state B. Data collection occurred over 3 sessions on separate days, each containing 4 runs of 18 trials. Participants provided self-reported valence and arousal ratings for each trial. Methods are detailed in Daly et al. (2018): Daly, I., Nicolaou, N., Williams, D., Hwang, F., Kirke, A., Miranda, E., Nasuto, S.J., 'Neural and physiological data from participants listening to affective music', Scientific Data, 2018.
